content.full_text:
December 19, 1991

Blanca Breeze
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 300
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Breeze:

Re: Written Confirmation on Accounts

I would like to request written confirmation of my understanding of the following based on our telephone conversation on December 12, 1991:

A Los Angeles City officeholder can maintain two separate accounts. A campaign account for future elections and one, originating from the campaign account of the past election, which can now be used for current office expenses. This current office account can be used by Los Angeles officeholders for officeholder expenses and legal defense funds. This advice was based on the April 7, 1989 letter from John Wallace, FPPC legal counsel, to Randy Riddle, Deputy City Attorney of the City and County of San Francisco. (File No. I-89-016).

I appreciate your assistance in this matter. If you have any questions, please contact me.

Sincerely,

John F. Suggs
Management Analyst

JS:js
